finding: Queue-request prompt detection exists in both `packages/cli/src/commands/next/intent-normalizers.ts` and `packages/cli/src/commands/next/route-predicates.ts`; updating only the normalizer did not affect the active `next.ts` route decision, so a new regression kept failing until the second copy was updated.
expectedBehavior: Queue-request language should have one shared predicate source consumed by both intent normalization and route selection, with tests proving the reported `taskIntent.queueRequested` and actual route decision agree.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed on 2026-07-13: `isQueueRequestedPrompt` now has one source in `intent-normalizers.ts`, while `route-predicates.ts` imports and re-exports that helper for the `next.ts` route decision. `validate-prompt-scoped-next` now asserts backlog continuation prompts set `taskIntent.queueRequested` and route to batch.
followUp: Keep the prompt-scoped regression as the SSOT guard whenever queue-request language is expanded.
